sanskrit:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
transliteration: uddhared ātmanātmānaṃ nātmānam avasādayet | ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ ||
literal_meaning: One must lift up the self by the self; one must not let the self down. For the self alone is the friend of the self, and the self alone is the foe of the self.
contextual_meaning: This verse reframes the mind as an internal companion that can either rescue or sabotage us, placing agency squarely within the individual. It warns that the same mental apparatus that can elevate a person toward clarity and liberation can, if left untrained, become the very force that drags them into despair and compulsive patterns.
psychological_state: A person caught in compulsive self-criticism who simultaneously senses they alone hold the key to their own rescue.
trigger_scenario: After a relapse into addictive behavior, someone stares at the mirror, torn between the promise of recovery and the crushing voice that says they will never change.
anchor_text: Be your own best friend, not your worst enemy.
